# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with EBS volumes for application data

Parameters:
  EnvironmentName:
    Type: String
    Default: dev
    AllowedPattern: '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$'
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - t3.xlarge
      - m5.large
      - m5.xlarge
  AmiId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Image::Id
    Description: AMI for the application instances
  InstanceCount:
    Type: Number
    Default: 2
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 10
  RootVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 20
    MinValue: 8
  DataVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    MinValue: 1
  DataDeviceName:
    Type: String
    Default: /dev/sdf
  AppPort:
    Type: Number
    Default: 8080
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 65535
  SSHLocation:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    AllowedPattern: '^([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}\/([0-9]|[1-2][0-9]|3[0-2])$'

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for application EC2 instances
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref SSHLocation
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: !Ref AppPort
          ToPort: !Ref AppPort
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-sg'

  AppInstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-instance-role-${AWS::StackName}'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-instance-role'

  AppInstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref AppInstanceRole
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-instance-profile-${AWS::StackName}'

  AppLaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-lt-${AWS::StackName}'
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: !Ref AmiId
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
        KeyName: !Ref KeyName
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Name: !Ref AppInstanceProfile
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
        BlockDeviceMappings:
          - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
              VolumeType: gp3
              DeleteOnTermination: true
        TagSpecifications:
          - ResourceType: instance
            Tags:
              - Key: Name
                Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app'
              - Key: Environment
                Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
          - ResourceType: volume
            Tags:
              - Key: Environment
                Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
              - Key: Role
                Value: app-root
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: !Sub |
            #!/bin/bash
            set -euxo pipefail
            mkdir -p /app/data
            for i in {1..30}; do
              if [ -b /dev/nvme1n1 ]; then DEVICE=/dev/nvme1n1; break; fi
              if [ -b /dev/xvdf ]; then DEVICE=/dev/xvdf; break; fi
              sleep 2
            done
            if [ -n "${DEVICE:-}" ]; then
              if ! blkid ${DEVICE}; then
                mkfs -t xfs ${DEVICE}
              fi
              UUID=$(blkid -s UUID -o value ${DEVICE})
              grep -q "${UUID}" /etc/fstab || echo "UUID=${UUID} /app/data xfs defaults,nofail 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
              mount -a
            fi

  AppAutoScalingGroup:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      MinSize: !Ref InstanceCount
      MaxSize: !Ref InstanceCount
      DesiredCapacity: !Ref InstanceCount
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref SubnetId
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref AppLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt AppLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app'
          PropagateAtLaunch: true
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

  DataVolumeAttachmentWaitHandle:
    Type: AWS::CloudFormation::WaitConditionHandle

  AppDataVolume1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !GetAtt AppInstance1.AvailabilityZone
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSize
      VolumeType: gp3
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-data-1'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  AppDataVolume2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Condition: CreateSecondVolume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !GetAtt AppInstance2.AvailabilityZone
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSize
      VolumeType: gp3
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-data-2'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  AppInstance1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref AmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref AppInstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-1'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash
          set -euxo pipefail
          mkdir -p /app/data
          for i in {1..30}; do
            if [ -b /dev/nvme1n1 ]; then DEVICE=/dev/nvme1n1; break; fi
            if [ -b /dev/xvdf ]; then DEVICE=/dev/xvdf; break; fi
            sleep 2
          done
          if [ -n "${DEVICE:-}" ]; then
            if ! blkid ${DEVICE}; then
              mkfs -t xfs ${DEVICE}
            fi
            UUID=$(blkid -s UUID -o value ${DEVICE})
            grep -q "${UUID}" /etc/fstab || echo "UUID=${UUID} /app/data xfs defaults,nofail 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
            mount -a
          fi

  AppInstance2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref AmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref AppInstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-2'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash
          set -euxo pipefail
          mkdir -p /app/data
          for i in {1..30}; do
            if [ -b /dev/nvme1n1 ]; then DEVICE=/dev/nvme1n1; break; fi
            if [ -b /dev/xvdf ]; then DEVICE=/dev/xvdf; break; fi
            sleep 2
          done
          if [ -n "${DEVICE:-}" ]; then
            if ! blkid ${DEVICE}; then
              mkfs -t xfs ${DEVICE}
            fi
            UUID=$(blkid -s UUID -o value ${DEVICE})
            grep -q "${UUID}" /etc/fstab || echo "UUID=${UUID} /app/data xfs defaults,nofail 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
            mount -a
          fi

  AppDataVolumeAttachment1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      Device: !Ref DataDeviceName
      InstanceId: !Ref AppInstance1
      VolumeId: !Ref AppDataVolume1

  AppDataVolumeAttachment2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Condition: CreateSecondVolume
    Properties:
      Device: !Ref DataDeviceName
      InstanceId: !Ref AppInstance2
      VolumeId: !Ref AppDataVolume2

Conditions:
  CreateSecondInstance: !Not [!Equals [!Ref InstanceCount, 1]]
  CreateSecondVolume: !Not [!Equals [!Ref InstanceCount, 1]]

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Value: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
  Instance1Id:
    Value: !Ref AppInstance1
  Instance2Id:
    Condition: CreateSecondInstance
    Value: !Ref AppInstance2
  DataVolume1Id:
    Value: !Ref AppDataVolume1
  DataVolume2Id:
    Condition: CreateSecondVolume
    Value: !Ref AppDataVolume2
